---
engine: markdown
title: "Britain's Top Baby Names Are Losing Their Grip"
description: "Baby names registered in England & Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland, showing how the ten most popular names' share of births has roughly halved since the 1990s for both boys and girls."
date: "2026-06-16"
order: 20260616
image: images/featured-light.png
image-alt: "Three line-chart panels, one each for England & Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland, showing the top-ten baby names' share of recorded births. In every panel a teal line for boys and an amber line for girls fall from roughly a quarter of births in the 1990s to about a tenth by 2024, and Scotland's panel reaches back to 1974 with the longest, steepest decline."
categories: [2026]
---

```{typst}
//| include: false
//| file: _plot.typ
//| output-filename: featured.png
//| format: png
//| dpi: 300
//| width: 18cm
//| height: 9.45cm
```

```{typst}
//| label: fig-2026-06-16
//| echo: true
//| code-fold: true
//| code-summary: "See the code that generated this plot."
//| file: _plot.typ
//| output-filename: plot.svg
//| width: 18cm
//| height: 9.45cm
//| fig-alt: "{{< meta image-alt >}}"
//| fig-cap: "Top-ten baby names' share of recorded births in England & Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland, by sex, from 1974 to 2025."
```

Data: [TidyTuesday 2026-06-16](https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/tree/main/data/2026/2026-06-16).

In every UK nation the ten most popular names now claim a far smaller share of births than they did a generation ago.
The share has roughly halved since the 1990s, sliding to around a tenth of all babies, and the fall holds for both boys and girls.
Parents are drawing from an ever wider pool, so no single name dominates the way it once did.

Scotland's record reaches back to 1974, far earlier than the 1996 and 1997 starts for England & Wales and Northern Ireland, and its decline is the longest and steepest on show.
The three nations set out from different heights but end up close together, all converging on a more fragmented set of names.
